title.rendered: Golf Outing Raises Nearly $1 Million for Joe Burrow Foundation

content.rendered:
A sold-out field of golfers and celebrities golfed in the Inaugural Joe Burrow Foundation Golf Invitational raising $924,250 for the organization at Coldstream Country Club on June 5, 2023.

The Joe Burrow Foundation Golf Invitational was presented by Gravity Diagnostics, Encore Technologies and Jeff Ruby Culinary Entertainment, and was hosted by Jimmy and Robin Burrow, the parents of Joe.

Celebrities who participated in the golf outing included Cincinnati Bengals Head Coach Zac Taylor, along with Bengals coaches and staff: Brian Callahan, Darrin Simmons, Dan Pitcher, Brad Kragthorpe, Lou Anarumo, Doug Rosfeld and Sam Francis.

Also joining for the golf outing were LSU Head Coach Brian Kelly, ESPN’s Kirk Herbstreit, NFL Hall of Famer Anthony Muñoz, Bengals Legends Kenny Anderson, Jim Breech and David Fulcher, Bengals Broadcasters Dan Hoard and Dave Lapham, Hall of Fame Broadcaster Marty Brennaman, and Cincinnati Reds Legend George Foster.

The event committee led by Amy Floyd, executive director of the Joe Burrow Foundation, included: Chrissie Blatt, Briana Warm, Maggie Luggen, Amy Craft, Mikayla Pittman and Andi Sempier.

At the Post-Party celebration, nearly $500,000 was raised from the live and silent auction hosted by Cincinnati native Bill Hemmer and FOX News anchor. In attendance from the Cincinnati Bengals team were Joe Mixon, Orlando Brown Jr., Ted Karras and Sam Hubbard.

The Joe Burrow Foundation provides resources and support to the underprivileged and the underserved in Ohio and Louisiana. The Foundation launched in October 2022 to help families who are working to overcome food insecurity and childhood mental health issues. You can also follow us on Facebook, Instagram and Twitter.
